semantic.role: Abstract base class defining the standardized execution skeleton for ArchSpine pipeline tasks. Orchestrates core engines (Scanner, Manifest, Aggregator, OutputManager, RuleEngine, ContextEngine, ASTExtractor) within a lifecycle that enforces pipeline contracts and shared state management.
responsibilities: Provide a reusable, type-safe abstract class for all pipeline tasks.
Define the execution lifecycle contract (likely via a template method such as `execute`).
Coordinate the invocation order and data flow between Scanner, Manifest, Aggregator, OutputManager, RuleEngine, ContextEngine, and ASTExtractor.
Expose a `TaskContext` interface that encapsulates shared state and artifacts throughout task execution.
Allow concrete task implementations to specialize input/output types and override steps.
outOfScope: CLI command definitions, argument parsing, and user interaction (handled by entry-point modules).
Configuration loading or environment setup (delegated to infrastructure).
Implementation of specific scanning, aggregation, or rule logic — those reside in respective engine modules.
Persistent storage implementation (execution checkpoint store is typed but not operated here).
HTTP handlers, MCP tool definitions, or other external interfaces.
invariants: Core pipeline isolation: This module must not depend on CLI entry code or command-specific logic.
All tasks must adhere to the input/output type contract defined by the generic parameters.
The execution lifecycle must be consistent across all concrete SpineTask implementations.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: To provide a stable, reusable skeleton for all ArchSpine pipeline tasks, enforcing consistent orchestration of core engines and shared state management.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Initial commit establishing the core pipeline task abstraction.

publicSurface.description: Abstract base class for ArchSpine pipeline tasks. Consumers extend this class to implement concrete tasks and invoke its public execution method to run the pipeline orchestration.
Interface describing the shared context object that flows through task execution. It holds state such as task artifacts, view ID, and checkpoint information.

ruleViolations.reason: No violation. The file imports only from engine and infrastructure modules and does not reference any CLI entry code.
